title: cassebeera ternifolia
intro: Cassebeera ternifolia, also known as the Cassia auriculata, is a flowering plant species in the family Fabaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antidiabetic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Cassia auriculata include reducing blood sugar levels, managing diabetes, and alleviating symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Cassia auriculata can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
